honest: Productive but fragmented, like context-switching between three different brain states.
summary: Prepped for a tech interview while juggling product design for an AI coach and competitor analysis, with a brief detour into MetaGlass SDK setup.
lessons: Dark navy/teal with amber makes slides pop without being distracting.
Direct push to main works when the change is small and scoped.
Real SDK docs often drift from bundled skill documentation.
Intersection strategy beats competing on single features.
Start users with small actions before showing the full plan.

items.title: interviewPrep
items.body: I was trying to create a 14-slide presentation for a 45-minute project deep dive interview based on the IDG revamp tech design document.
decisions: Used dark navy/teal color palette with amber accents
Structured slides to match interview format: problem → architecture → implementation → evaluation → impact
Added dedicated "Failures, Tradeoffs & Lessons" slide
Incorporated comparison tables, stat callouts, and version timeline
Ensured each slide had visual elements and consistent layouts

items.title: Fetch yage newsletter
items.body: I was trying to fetch the latest newsletter from yage-ai.kit.com and save it to my SOP folder.
decisions: Ran the fetch script as specified
Script successfully discovered and downloaded new posts
Converted posts to markdown format
Saved to newsletters/yage-ai/ directory

items.title: Check files.zip for next task
items.body: I was trying to refine the AI goal coach product design after a brainstorming session.
decisions: Shape B as the main product with AI coach as a sidecar
Use forms for initial onboarding, not chat
Start users with a small first action before showing full plan
Keep one primary plan with optional background habits
Generalize the seed plan to be universally health-positive
open: Need to write the actual PRD document
Need to conduct fresh competitive analysis for V2
Need to implement the plan schema and data migration

items.title: Analyze competitors for product-management competitive brief
items.body: I wanted to analyze competitors for Cadence's family habit and purine food tracking app to identify differences and shared features.
decisions: Focused on two MVP features: family-shared habit check-ins and purine food lookup
Identified direct competitors across habit apps and gout-specific apps
Analyzed key differentiators: read-only family sharing, combined habit+food database, bilingual support, PWA-first approach
Recommended protecting the intersection strategy rather than competing on single axes
open: Should we prioritize adding family sharing to counter 痛风快好's potential threat?
How to maintain lifestyle tone while expanding food database?
What's the plan for monitoring competitor moves in the gout+habit space?

items.title: Push local code to remote
items.body: I was trying to push my local code to the remote repository.
decisions: Pushed staged changes to remote
Chose direct push to main over feature branch

items.title: Set up MetaGlass SDK and explore app ideas
items.body: Set up MetaGlass SDK and explore app ideas for building a language learning app.
decisions: Installed Meta DAT Claude skills into `.claude/skills/`
Scaffolding "Look & Learn" language immersion app as prototype
Using XcodeGen to generate Xcode project with SPM dependencies
Real SDK API is async (v0.6.0), not matching bundled skill docs
open: Swap StubVisionLabeler for real VLM (Claude Vision via anthropic SDK)
Test on real hardware via Meta AI registration flow
Add SRS scheduling and export-to-Anki button for retention/paywall hook
